_patch_meta.description: Session B completion patch for registry 163 (trust). Sets session_b_status to Session B Complete. Analysis document: WA-163-trust-analysis-20260326.docx (6 parts). 47 working terms analysed. Key families: batach (trust verb), betach (security), chasah/machseh (refuge), shaan (lean/rely), qavah/tiqvah (await/hope), yachal (hope in darkness), aman/emunah/emet, peithō/pepoithēsis, pisteuō/pistis/pistos, elpizō. Key finding: trust is defined by its object; biblical trust is a posture of the whole inner person — rest, leaning, sheltering, waiting. Eighth consecutive applicator anomaly corrected by OP-001. Largest bleed of the session: 129 terms deleted including galal/chul/nasa/shalom families.

operations.description: RECURRING APPLICATOR ANOMALY — eighth consecutive registry (98, 34, 160, 126, 59, 42, 23, 163). update_registry ops in PREANALYSIS patches not executing. OP-001 corrects registry 163 directly. Claude Code must resolve this before any further session work.

Session B analysis complete for registry 163 (trust). Document: WA-163-trust-analysis-20260326.docx. Working set: 47 terms (20 extracted, 27 extracted_thin | Hebrew 34, Greek 13). Cross-registry: pisteuō/pistis family shared with faith (59). Largest bleed correction in current session: 129 terms deleted. Programme flags: PH2-163-001 (G4100 cross-registry), PH2-163-002 (H5921A — eighth consecutive extraction anomaly).
